programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.desc: Susan gets stranded in a small town, where she is aghast to learn her former fiance, believed to be dead, is actually alive and well - presenting Dr Sloan and Jesse with a perplexing mystery to unravel. Dick Van Dyke stars(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A bank at Quantico is robbed and a security guard killed, but the team soon discovers that stealing money may not have been the perpetrators' primary objective. Meanwhile, Gibbs is given the task of evaluating an inexperienced new agent assigned by Vance to help investigate the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rey Valentin(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs sends DiNozzo and Ziva to retrieve top-secret information from a secure military base - a mission aimed at catching a mole in the NCIS ranks. Unfortunately, it soon becomes clear that the traitor is still at large - so the building is locked down and all agents are rounded up in a final effort to catch the culprit. Guest starring Jude Ciccolella(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Having identified the mole in their ranks, the agents arrest the traitor's contact Ted Bankston, a former NSA agent working for a kidnapper code-named the Weatherman - who is making millions on the stock markets out of stolen military data. Drama, with Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Cote de Pablo(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed in what initially seems to be a road-rage incident - until investigations reveal evidence of foul play. Digging deeper, the team finds the dead man belonged to an underground fight club, and sets about discovering whether his involvement was a factor in the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: As Christmas approaches, the team works overtime to solve a robbery - a case made doubly difficult by the fact that the prime suspect is a former petty officer who has supposedly been dead for 17 years. Eventually arrested at a Vietnam memorial, he manages to convince Gibbs of his innocence - but a discovery in the forensics lab suggests he may be guilty after all. Guest starring Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: A plane crashes into a pylon and lands on a busy street during an exercise, killing two civilians - and witnesses later claim the pilot was showing off. David James Elliott stars(n)

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: A traumatised Vietnam War veteran arrives at JAG headquarters to report he has just murdered a PoW camp commandant, but Harm is initially sceptical of his claims - until a body is discovered(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Agents G Callen and Sam Hanna investigate after a naval officer is killed during an LAPD shootout, with two other men who died in the gun battle being identified as members of a Mexican drug cartel. Spin-off drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects, an elite NCIS division specialising in undercover assignments. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: On a live-fire air force training exercise, someone hacks into the control systems of a drone and fires one of its two live missiles, striking and killing a serviceman, before piloting it away. Sam and Callen meet with a member of the National Security Agency, while Eric's inquiries lead him to a group of students on an advanced computer science course and Kensi is sent under cover to pose as its guest speaker(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A man who stole a car and ran down and killed one of his pursuers before escaping is identified as Walton Flynn, an ex-marine now working for a private security firm in Iraq. Sam and Callen learn that the businessman he had been hired to protect had been murdered and that the people chasing him were hired by his employers to dish out their own form of justice. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: Ultimate Force
programme.desc: During an exercise at a nuclear power station, Henno and Dave are ambushed by Russian nationalists who seize a container of cobalt rods they use to build a bomb. As the government calls a crisis meeting and London is sealed off, the activists take their detonators to the roof of a tower block - but after Finn realises the device they can see is a decoy, the team face a race against time to locate the real bomb(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The decomposing body of a young woman is found in the bathroom of a New York hotel, having been undisturbed for three days. When Avery Ryan's and her team investigate, they discover the victim seemed to have been ordering room service three days after her death, and her messages to staff had released vicious malware that eradicated several hours of surveillance footage from the hotel's security cameras. The chain of evidence leads Ryan to a hacker thought to have been on-site at the time of the woman's death, but as Nelson and Ramirez continue digging for clues, it becomes clear this case is far from closed. Patricia Arquette stars(n)

programme.desc: Avery Ryan's old friend Jordan Tan seeks her help in locating his missing teenage daughter Zoey. When the team looks into high-schooler's online history, they discover she had been the target of hacks, faked phone calls and social media posts, as well as a malicious website titled 'Kill Yourself Zoey Tan'. The spate of abuse bears all the hallmarks of a particularly nasty case of online bullying, but Zoey's digital footprint offers the investigators a lead in the form of online boyfriend Owen, who lives 300 miles away and seems to have invited her to visit. However, when the team ventures to Owen's home, they discover his invitation was just another facet of the hate campaign, and with Zoey's whereabout still unknown, serious concerns are raised about her wellbeing. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette, with guests Byron Mann and BooBoo Stewart(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Kerry Young's funeral casts a sombre shadow over Sun Hill as the team gather to pay their last respects to their colleague, but any hopes of a dignified send-off are dashed by an emotional outburst from Gabriel. Debt-ridden Jim fears an investigation into illegal goings-on at the greyhound track will expose his own complicity, while the close professional relationship between Jack and Debbie turns personal(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Debbie is assigned a major drugs investigation - much to Phil's irritation and Jim's alarm - but the chance to prove herself takes second place to family concerns when she learns her son has sustained internal bleeding from his fall. The bad news prompts her to hand in her resignation, leaving Jack wondering what the future holds for their relationship. Andrea tells Smithy that Gabriel raped Kerry, whose death continues to take its toll on the renegade copper(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Kerry's father visits Sun Hill to collect his murdered daughter's belongings, reopening old wounds for Smithy who begins to see Gabriel for the manipulative psychopath he is and threatens to officially accuse him of rape. Elsewhere, Gary thinks he's stumbled across a dismembered body - until Suzie points out the grisly remains are actually a collection of prosthetic limbs(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The offer of a substantial reward inspires Hetty to find the bright spark behind a spate of arson attacks, and she begins by investigating the activities of a neighbour's foster daughter whose absences from home coincide with the fires. Housewife-detective series, starring Patricia Routledge, with Derek Benfield and Dominic Monaghan(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The detectives find themselves swamped with work after their recent success. Hetty turns her attention to a troubled village, while Robert and Geoffrey embark on a case of their own. Private eye drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield(n)

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Cinder Path
programme.desc: Feature-length drama about one man's journey through love and war in the early part of the 20th century. Charlie MacFell returns from university to the family farm, where his strict father still rules with an iron fist - a situation that soon changes thanks to a misunderstanding with a foolish farmhand. Lloyd Owen stars, with Catherine Zeta-Jones, Tom Bell and Maria Mile(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives are busy preparing for Sister Evangelina's surprise jubilee celebrations, marking 50 years since she took her vows, but a face from the past threatens to ruin the party for the guest of honour. It's back to business when a young woman with Down's syndrome is brought in feeling unwell. To everyone's surprise, they discover she is six months pregnant, causing some of them to think the worst, and the staff are joined by new nurse Patsy, whose sense of humour and no-nonsense attitude make her an instant hit(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie is chuffed when local curate Tom invites her to join him for a day of cricket, sending her to work with a spring in her step. Is love on the cards for the young midwife? Shelagh's infertility has made her think long and hard about what she wants in life, so she approaches her husband with an idea, while Patsy upsets a young mother in the clinic and is moved to the district rota, where she works tirelessly to diagnose a docker's unusual symptoms(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry and the team agree to help a man who believes his wife is still alive, even though she apparently died 18 months earlier in a car accident. They decide the best way to convince him is to have her exhumed - only to be faced with more questions than answers when it turns out the body is not hers after all. Meanwhile, Brian needs to find a handyman when Esther bans him from his latest DIY project. Hugh Dennis and Samuel West guest star, with Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: American diplomat Ryan Reed is shot dead and the team arrives at the scene to find his body posed - sitting upright on a bench with his hand tucked inside his jacket. An argument quickly ensues between the police and the US embassy, which claims the murder falls under its jurisdiction, while the Lyell crew fights against the bureaucracy of the FBI, aided only by the US deputy chief of mission Matt Garcia. However, when he goes missing, Nikki is concerned he might be the next target(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Part one of two. The body of a Falklands War veteran who became one of the leading lights in the campaign against nuclear weapons is found in a stretch of river running underneath the Ministry of Defence. As Boyd and the team set about investigating the man's former allies, they stumble upon footage of an incident intelligence services had tried to cover up - and Grace is reunited with a former lover from her own time in the anti-nuclear movement. Drama, starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston, with Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe, Into the Storm), John McArdle (Brookside) and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral, Spooks)(n)

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Anna Westin, a troubled childhood friend of Wallander's daughter, visits his house late at night begging for his help - but disappears soon afterwards. Meanwhile, an elderly woman's burned body is found in a shallow grave, and when the detective discovers a connection between Anna and the murder victim, he fears she could be the next target of a dangerous religious fanatic. Linda returns to Ystad to help with the investigation, and has life-changing news for her father. Guest starring Lindsay Duncan (Rome), with Kenneth Branagh and Jeany Spark(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Crystal Palace at Etihad Stadium and Watford v Tottenham Hotspur at Vicarage Road. Plus, Arsenal v Sheffield United, Brighton & Hove Albion v Aston Villa, Norwich City v Bournemouth, Southampton v Wolverhampton Wanderers, West Ham United v Everton and Newcastle United v Chelsea(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, featuring a badly damaged suspended chair from the 1960s, a cast iron statue of Roman god Hercules, and a smokers' stand(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live on coral reefs, including groupers, small predators that scientists believe are as intelligent as chimpanzees. These creatures have a remarkable aptitude for communication to the point where they can interact with other species including octopi and work together to catch prey. Plus, footage of a vast spawning event involving several different species of fish, coral and invertebrates, and time-lapse photography of coral polyps growing and dying, creating a structure visible from space(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Ballycastle in Co Antrim, a coastal community that is becoming known for its crafts and artisan food and drink. Margherita Taylor heads to a farm that produces ethical and sustainable produce, Joe Crowley catches up with a couple who make award-winning smoked salmon and dulse seaweed, and Charlotte Smith goes on a red squirrel safari with a school group(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: It is 1903 and on the edge of Niagara Falls, something is wrong at Nikola Tesla's generator plant, where someone - or something - is sabotaging the maverick inventor's work. Has Tesla really received a message from Mars? And where does his great rival Thomas Edison fit into these events? The Doctor and her companions Yaz, Ryan and Graham must join forces with one of history's greatest minds to save both him and planet Earth. Guest starring Goran Visnjic, Robert Glenister and Anjli Mohindra(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Lucille is seconded to St Cuthbert's when a ward sister is off sick, but a lift malfunction sees her spend the evening with mother-to-be Connie instead of her planned rendezvous with Cyril. Nurse Crane tries to help a woman who is newly arrived from East Pakistan, while Shelagh enlists Violet, Trixie, Sister Frances and Valerie to put on a charity fashion show to raise money for the incubator fund(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Journalists from around the world descend on the Old Bailey as Stephen Ward's trial begins and the doctor trusts his friends in high places will come forward in support of his character. While Stephen faces the full force of the law and Mandy thrives on the publicity, Christine is terrified that her lie about Lucky Gordon will be revealed at the appeal. Starring Sophie Cookson and James Norton(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Liverpool v Manchester United at Anfield and Burnley v Leicester City at Turf Moor. Liverpool have been in dominant form this term, but United were the first team to take points off them this season when October's reverse fixture ended in a 1-1 draw, while Leicester will have been looking to capitalise on any slip-up from Jurgen Klopp's men with a victory over the Clarets(n)

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Inside Out London
programme.desc: The return of the programme focusing on stories of interest to the capital. Abi Jaiyeola investigates the company charging thousands for training courses in how to buy property but which leaves many of its customers with nothing but debt, while naturalist Mike Dilger finds out if a pair of busy beavers can save a village from flooding. Sean Fletcher visits the farms popping up high above and deep below the streets of London(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is mortified to realise she has forgotten Mick's birthday and quickly arranges a surprise party - and as everyone gathers in the pub to celebrate, she finds the temptation to have a drink hard to resist. Phil is celebrating his birthday too with a family meal and is relieved when Louise agrees to attend - only for her to lash out at him and Ben. Kat is thrilled when Whitney sets up a meeting with a new client, Rainie targets Jean and Daniel, and Ian tries to persuade Ruby to let him use the club for his election winner's party(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The body of a woman is found on train tracks in London, badly damaged from electrical burns. On the mortuary slab, the woman's recent and historic injuries suggest years of abuse, likely within the home. It is a difficult post-mortem for Nikki, as it stirs up troubling memories from her childhood. First of a two-part episode of the long-running crime drama following the work of the Lyell Centre's team of forensic pathology experts, starring Emilia Fox, David Caves, Richard Lintern and Liz Carr(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: After a bad day at work, Liam talks to Jess and is forced to confront his mental health problems, leading him to remember the conflicting advice of two of his old teachers. At school he found little emotional support from PE teacher Mr Stones, but unconventional substitute teacher Miss Monroe made him try to channel his anger into more creative pursuits(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Hal Cruttenden hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, warming up the audience before comedy circuit regulars Justin Moorhouse and Tom Stade take the mic(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Michael B Jordan discuss starring in legal drama Just Mercy, while actress and comedian Jennifer Saunders talks about new mystery thriller series The Stranger, in which she features. Plus, Patrick Stewart reflects on his decision to reprise his most famous role in latest sci-fi spin-off Star Trek: Picard. Michael Kiwanuka performs Hero and more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Reports on how the end of free prescription deliveries is affecting vulnerable and elderly people, and a scam that is using a respected high street name to deceive people(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a terraced house in Wigston, Leicestershire, a flat in Swanscombe, Kent, and a piece of land in Bolton, Lancashire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: A couple from Holmfirth in West Yorkshire have an empty nest now their children have left home and wonder if the time has come to realise their dream of moving to New Zealand(n)

programme.desc: A jeweller takes the biggest risk of his life when he is confronted by an armed robber. A thief makes a schoolboy error while trying to steal a cash machine from a garage(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Thomas Plant help two teams search for valuable items at the British Motor Museum in Gaydon, Warwickshire, while Charlie Ross meets ex-racing driver Damon Hill(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma and Rob begin to suspect Jimmi's father may have framed him, and Valerie connects with her Jewish heritage, with a little help from Rabbi David(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A new musical director for the bell-ringers of St Marys leads to discord, disharmony and the blood-curdling sound of murder. Mystery, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin meets a couple of creative Californians keen to make a lifestyle leap to the Welsh borders, and also discovers how llamas are thriving in the British countryside(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheep farmers head to Ryedale to show off the finest animals in their flocks, including a retired businessman who is hoping to carry on his wife's legacy(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth look for bargains in Scotland, visiting the largest lowland loch and getting a crash course in chemistry before attending an auction at Kinbuck(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Linda is humiliated when Mick describes her actions the previous night - but their argument is cut short by shocking news from Ollie's school. As if that were not enough for the troubled landlady, she also faces Sharon, who wants to know why she said Keanu was alive. Meanwhile, Lisa tells Phil she and Louise are moving to Portugal - a plan he opposes - Ian frantically tries to get his fellow residents to vote for him in the election, and Daniel and Jean run rings around Rainie and Stuart(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Serena needs to avoid causing trouble with an inspection looming, but when Jason has problems with the privatised porter company, she must decide if she can afford to help him. Nicky finds herself having to treat her own mother, who has been involved in a road accident, and is forced to take desperate measures to help her. Essie realises that she has to tell Ben everything about the illegal adoption if they are to stay together, but is not sure she can trust him with the truth(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. The investigation into Jenny's murder grinds to a halt when it emerges that she was dead before her body was left on the tracks, but the women at the refuge demand answers. Thomas makes some friends in high places who offer to help him achieve his political ambitions - but there is a price to be paid for their support(n)

programme.title: Veganville
programme.desc: The group sets up a stall at a local farmers' market, offering visitors a taste of vegan food - as well as a toy dog on a barbecue smothered in fake blood. Joey has an encounter with a woman who wants to find a way to provide nutritious and affordable meals for her two children, and the meeting leads him to reassess his approach to activism(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Asian Odyssey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Nadiya Hussain takes a trip to the Himalayan country of Nepal to learn more about its people, places and food as part of her adventure to explore her identity. She begins in Kathmandu, where she joins a supper club run by young foodies who share with her the vastly varied cuisine of their country, then heads to the historic city of Bhaktapur, which was devastated by the earthquake of 2015, and visits an abbey to meet nuns from a Buddhist sect who practise kung fu and teach female empowerment(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Danny Clarke head to Croydon to create exotic, Jamaican-style garden for owner Carmen, who needs a tranquil space to remember her lost daughter(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don brings his globe-trotting botanical tour to a close with a visit to South East Asia, where he hopes to witness a true tropical oasis. Visiting the immaculately presented garden of Jim Thompson House in Bangkok, he discovers an exotic plot with a Western flavour. He also meets former president Lee Kuan Yew, who once stated that Singapore should be 'a city in a garden'(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Welney Wildlife Reserve in Norfolk, where Charlotte Smith helps out with a swan census and Steve Brown is on the trail of the elusive cranes(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The horticulturalist explores gardens of early independent America, learning how the history of the South is still very much evident in the places and people he meets. He visits the famous garden where President Thomas Jefferson, one of America's founding fathers, enjoyed spending his time seeking out seeds and plants for his 1,000-ft vegetable garden. From there he heads to Charleston, which boasts some of the most beautiful trees in the world and learns about the life of the plantations there. Later, in Miami, Monty visits one of the most spectacular Renaissance recreations built on marshland(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: Mary visits Powderham Castle in Devon to spend time with the Earl and Countess of Devon and their two young children, who are reinventing the Earldom for the 21st century. Mary's time with the family reveals stories of romance and tragedy as she discovers more of this extraordinary home. In the castle's historic kitchen, Mary prepares a Devon cream tea, before making a delicate peach posset for a Midsummer Eve woodland party the family are throwing to celebrate their two year anniversary at the castle(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes Daniel O'Donnell back to the islands of Arranmore and Owey off the coast of Co Donegal, where the singer reminisces about a childhood holiday he had there in 1968(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening eight frames of the final from Alexandra Palace, London, where the best-of-19-frames match takes place. Judd Trump dominated last year's corresponding session, racing into a 7-1 lead against seven-time champion Ronnie O'Sullivan. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Michael Baggott assess items at Barrow Hill Roundhouse in Derbyshire. Presenter Paul Martin visits nearby Renishaw Hall to learn about the Sitwell Trio - three siblings who took the artistic and literary world by storm in the early 20th century(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Action from the World Cup men's downhill in Lauberhorn, and the Laax Open snowboarding contest, both which take place in Switzerland(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the concluding session of the final from Alexandra Palace, London, played over the best-of-19-frames. After going into the session facing a 7-1 deficit, Ronnie O'Sullivan fought back by winning two of the opening three frames, but Judd Trump was eventually able to prevail 10-4. It was the first time 'The Juddernaut' had won this title, and he went on to win the World Championship in the May of what was a memorable 2019 for him. The Bristolian also added the World Grand Prix title to his résumé, along with the International Championship, World Open and Northern Ireland Open later in the year. In November, he reached the final of the Champion of Champions event in Coventry, but lost 10-9 to Neil Robertson in a high-quality encounter. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Son of Saul (2015)
programme.desc: A Jewish prisoner in a Nazi concentration camp is forced to assist with the genocide being carried out. When he finds his own son's body, he vows to give the boy a proper burial instead of allowing his remains to be burned. As he looks for a rabbi to perform the ceremony, his fellow prisoners plot an uprising. Drama, starring Geza Rohrig and Levente Molnar. In Hungarian, Yiddish, German, Russian, Polish, French, Greek and Slovak(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Liverpool, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Properties in St Albans, Hertfordshire, are judged by neighbours, beginning with a 1930s space-saving conversion, a Victorian terrace, and a traditional two-up, two-down(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac and Kian return to work and support each other as they try to conquer their demons, and Sacha's feelings for Essie are only being made worse by living together. Meanwhile, Serena tries to find a new role for herself, but with Jason coping well and a new face to contend with, she may find herself forced out(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer set sail on the Solent for an Isle of Wight antiques hunt. Both uncover silver goodies, but Charlie Ross manages to rip his trousers. At the auction at Battle, East Sussex, Izzie's silver brooch goes against Charlie's silver pen(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hardworking families across the UK who work around the clock to make their farms a success compete to win the much coveted and prestigious title of best farming family business at the British Farming Awards. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs compete in the last quarter-final, inventing a dish that uses squash as the key ingredient. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - Jay Rayner, Jimi Famurewa and Tracey MacLeod. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in Knockout Week(n)

programme.title: This Week in Parliament
programme.desc: Highlights of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

Paul Foster and Alex Marshall won the pairs last year, while Anderson is the defending singles champion. With commentary from David Corkill and John Price(n)

programme.desc: Raj Bisram and David Harper assess items brought to Allerton Castle in North Yorkshire, while Paul Martin finds out about Harrogate's connection with Russia's last tsarina(n)

programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the east of England in the inter-war period, with the first leg of his journey beginning in Canterbury and ending at Alexandra Palace in London(n)

programme.title: The Twinstitute
programme.desc: Doctors Chris and Xand van Tulleken challenge identical twins to help find out the best way to get fit, beat nausea and discover if you really can enhance brain performance. Fran and Jess are busy mothers with five young kids between them, while Pete and Jem admit the last time they went into a gym was when they were at school. Team Xand are going for the slow and steady approach - 150 minutes of exercise a week at a moderate pace - while Team Chris are going all in, with 75 minutes of intense work(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contenders to brave the black chair, answering questions on the specialist subjects of The Book of Mormon, the fiction of Franz Kafka, Durham County Cricket Club and the Lord of the Rings film trilogy, before they each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Darksiders take on the Forrests. Both teams have won one game and lost another, so this is their last chance to stay in the tournament. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set consisting of theashes, GeorgeOsborn, theresamay, and johnlewis(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz concludes, with two teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure the last place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: War in the Blood
programme.desc: Documentary following two cancer patients who have volunteered as test subjects for an experimental treatment. CAR T-cell therapy has been described as the first step towards a cure - but 53-year-old Graham and 18-year-old Mahmoud may never reap the benefits, as their role is to allow scientists to monitor the side effects. The film examines the relationship between the patients and the clinical team, and how age and life experience affects their physical and emotional response to the treatment(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Selling Sex
programme.desc: Louis meets women legally providing sexual services, either to make a living or to supplement their income, potentially earning hundreds of pounds per hour. But rather than on the streets or in illegal brothels, these women sell sex from their own homes or hotels, utilising technology to share photos, make bookings and vet potential clients - making the exchange more accessible and, according to some, safer than the illegal alternatives. As Louis meets some of those participating in this new sexual economy, he explores whether selling sex can ever be a healthy way to make money(n)

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents action from the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the pairs final and a first-round singles match between Stewart Anderson and Daniel Salmon(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families of pig farmers are hoping to turn their dreams into a reality and win the top prizes with their precious livestock at the Royal Norfolk Show(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The remaining candidates head to 70 St Mary Axe - also known as the Can of Ham, a spectacular new 21-storey building in the heart of the City of London. There, they are interviewed by some of Alan Sugar's most trusted advisers, who are primed to pick apart their business plans. There's tough talking, turmoil and tears as bravado breaks, egos are eradicated and business plans bulldozed, before Alan decides which two candidates have done enough to earn a place in the final(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The two finalists take on their last challenge to win an investment from Lord Sugar, with contestants from the series returning to aid them in their endeavours. In an intensive three-day challenge, the finalists must create a new brand for their company, produce a digital billboard and make a television advert, before pitching to Lord Sugar and a room full of industry experts at London's City Hall. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is crowned the winner(n)

programme.desc: The team visits Welney Wildlife Reserve in Norfolk, where Charlotte Smith helps out with a swan census and Steve Brown is on the trail of the elusive cranes(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and newcomer Steven Moore start their hunt in the Scottish Borders, buying items on their journey to sell at auction in Crooklands, near Kendal in the Lake District(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two farming families hope their much-loved pygmy goats will bring them acclaim at the Mid Devon Show, but the competition is tough and there is a record number of goats in the ring(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace hosts the first round of Knockout week, as the 12 chefs to make it through the finals compete to stay in the contest. The chefs face an invention test in which they must not use any modern kitchen gadgets. The four weakest performing contestants then go through to a cook-off, to decide which two will be eliminated from the competition(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine presents an appeal on behalf of Parkinson's UK, which funds research, support networks and specialised nurses to help those with the condition(n)

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the mixed pairs final and a singles match. Last year saw English pair Robert Paxton and Ellen Falkner defeat Nicky Brett of England and Helen Carman of New Zealand. With commentary from David Corkill, John Price and Sian Honnor(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the Royal Marines Museum in Southsea, Hampshire, Katherine Higgins learns about the local corset industry and Charles Hanson hears all about cap ribbons worn by German navy men. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin hitches a ride on hovercraft to uncover the history of the clever craft that was designed, built, and is still used on the Solent(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the east of England en route to Lincolnshire, discovering how the Crossrail project is helping to conserve birds and taking a gelato cart to Southend(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Holby City stars Rosie Marcel and Bob Barret look for bargains in the Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire flatlands, with the expert advice of David Harper and Louise Gostelow. However, the road trip is jeopardised when a flooded river blocks the way to the antiques shops - but a detour leads Bob to a beloved location, where he learns about landscape gardening(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: A brother and sister team fish for oysters in a traditional wooden sailing boat on the Fal estuary - but this is not enough to support them, so now they plan to trade it in for a small trawler. Further down river in St Mawes, a veteran trawler captain reflects on a past brush with death and the changing face of his home town, as working boats give way to yachts(n)

programme.title: Chris Packham: 7.7 Billion People and Counting
programme.desc: The naturalist investigates the impact of a growing human population on the world in light of predictions that the number of humans could reach 10 billion by the year 2050. He considers the steps that will need to be taken for Earth's natural resources to support so many people, visiting a city in Brazil that is on the verge of running out of water and a community school in Nigeria that may present a possible solution. Featuring contributions from leading environmental experts including David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: It is another full house at Sam's, as she cooks risotto for guests invited and uninvited. Phil arrives with her new boyfriend, and Jeff arrives with his new girlfriend and a monkey. Comedy, with Pamela Adlon and guest starring Sharon Stone(n)

programme.title: The Archiveologists
programme.desc: Comedians Diane Morgan and Joe Wilkinson parody a 1980s safety video(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Jason Bell and Osi Umenyiora to look back at the Conference Championship matches(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Chelsea at Meadow Park and Birmingham City v Manchester City at SportNation.bet Stadium(n)

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: The presenter tells the story of the 17th-century residence in Derbyshire - the pleasure palace of playboy Cavalier and ambitious courtier William Cavendish. Through a collection of artworks and items held within the castle, Lucy uncovers a colourful tale, capturing the tensions of England in the 1600s that would eventually lead the nation to civil war(n)

programme.title: All Aboard! New Zealand by Rail, Sea and Land
programme.desc: Cameras take an epic overland journey, by train, boat and car through New Zealand's breathtaking landscapes. The trek beings in Auckland, but the city soon gives way to rolling pastures, volcanic extremes, tranquil waterways, the snowcapped grandeur of the Southern Alps and the beauty of Fiordland(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Portrait of a Lifetime
programme.desc: A programme celebrating Billy Connolly's 75th birthday and 50 years in the business, as three Scottish artists, John Byrne, Jack Vettriano and Rachel Maclean, each create a new portrait of the comedian and actor. As he sits with each artist, Billy talks about his remarkable career which has taken him from musician and stand-up to Hollywood star and national treasure(n)

programme.title: Scotland and the Klan
programme.desc: Throughout the 18th Century, hundreds of thousands of Scots emigrated to America. Neil Oliver travels more than 2,000 miles to examine links between racism today in the Deep South and the Scottish settlers that first occupied it(n)

programme.title: Bunkers, Brutalism and Bloodymindedness: Concrete Poetry with Jonathan Meades
programme.desc: Jonathan Meades presents a two-part documentary that celebrates 20th-century Brutalist architecture, which typically takes the form of block-like concrete constructions. In the first edition, he traces its origins in the Martial architecture of the Second World War, the Modern Gothic style - widely despised during the Victorian era - and even further back in the time of John Vanbrugh(n)

programme.title: Carved with Love: The Genius of British Woodwork
programme.desc: Profiles of influential figures in the world of wood craftsmanship, focusing on famous furniture designer Thomas Chippendale. The film traces his journey from humble roots working as a joiner in Otley, West Yorkshire, to his success as a cabinet-maker in London(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Portrait of a Lifetime
programme.desc: A programme celebrating Billy Connolly's 75th birthday and 50 years in the business, as three Scottish artists, John Byrne, Jack Vettriano and Rachel Maclean, each create a new portrait of the comedian and actor. As he sits with each artist, Billy talks about his remarkable career which has taken him from musician and stand-up to Hollywood star and national treasure(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: Architect and film-maker Francesco da Mosto sets off on a journey sailing from Venice in Italy to Istanbul in Turkey, retracing the trade routes his ancestors used in the 19th century. His first tasks are to research the voyage, learn vital new skills and gain the respect of his crew, with whom he will be spending the next few months at sea(n)

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins the last leg of his journey in Nicaragua and visits the Rama-Kriol people, who face losing their ancestral homes due to the construction of a new transoceanic waterway. Simon then discovers Hondurans living in the grip of some of the most violent criminal gangs in the world, with San Pedro Sula having the highest murder rate of any city on the planet. His journey ends in Jamaica, where he spends time with young people who have rejected gang life, offering a model of hope for future generations(n)

programme.title: A Fresh Guide to Florence with Fab 5 Freddy
programme.desc: Hip-hop pioneer Fred Brathwaite, better known to music fans as Fab 5 Freddy, who was part of a creative revolution during the 1970s and 1980s, goes back in time to the 15th and 16th centuries to examine the multi-racial society that played a part in the Italian Renaissance but have seemingly fallen through the cracks of history. With so many Renaissance artists representing black figures of prominence in their work, Freddy aims to bring focus back to the social change at the heart of the art movement and artists who were the cultural disrupters of their time(n)

programme.title: The Prosecutors
programme.desc: Documentary going behind-the-scenes of the Crown Prosecution Service, revealing how Britain's criminal justice system really works and what it takes to secure a conviction. In this edition, the prosecutors must decide how to charge a driver after a child is killed in a collision, with no evidence that he was drunk, speeding, or using his mobile phone. Another case sees the prosecutors select the right charge for an organised criminal gang that has been blowing up cash machines to steal money. Plus, community members challenge a CPS charging decision in a hate crime, where a man posted abusive messages on an extremist website(n)

programme.title: The Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines how Denmark emerged from modest beginnings to become one of the greatest powers and arbiters of taste in northern Europe. He tells a story of incredible transformation befitting the homeland of Hans Christian Andersen, creator of The Ugly Duckling and The Emperor's New Clothes among many other fairy tales(n)

programme.title: Britain in Focus: A Photographic History
programme.desc: In this series, Eamonn McCabe traces the story of British photography, from the rapid innovation of Roger Fenton in the 1800s to the satirical eye of Martin Parr in the 21st century. In the third and final episode, he focuses on the explosion of colour images in the late 1950s, to the ongoing impact of the digital revolution(n)

programme.title: Carved with Love: The Genius of British Woodwork
programme.desc: Paul Copley narrates the story of Grinling Gibbons, the 17th-century woodcarver who helped restore London to its former glory in the aftermath of the Great Fire. He reveals how Gibbons' masterpieces - created for the likes of Charles II and William of Orange - were held in such high regard he came to be known as 'the Michelangelo of wood'(n)

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins the last leg of his journey in Nicaragua and visits the Rama-Kriol people, who face losing their ancestral homes due to the construction of a new transoceanic waterway. Simon then discovers Hondurans living in the grip of some of the most violent criminal gangs in the world, with San Pedro Sula having the highest murder rate of any city on the planet. His journey ends in Jamaica, where he spends time with young people who have rejected gang life, offering a model of hope for future generations(n)

programme.title: A Fresh Guide to Florence with Fab 5 Freddy
programme.desc: Hip-hop pioneer Fred Brathwaite, better known to music fans as Fab 5 Freddy, who was part of a creative revolution during the 1970s and 1980s, goes back in time to the 15th and 16th centuries to examine the multi-racial society that played a part in the Italian Renaissance but have seemingly fallen through the cracks of history. With so many Renaissance artists representing black figures of prominence in their work, Freddy aims to bring focus back to the social change at the heart of the art movement and artists who were the cultural disrupters of their time(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull head to Northern Ireland, where they find out about the invention of the ejector seat, and visit Armagh Observatory, a railway museum located inside a barber shop, and SS Nomadic, known as Titanic's little sister(n)

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using new 3D technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Florence, and with the help of historian Ross King and the team, they discover the hidden secrets of Florence's great cathedral and its architectural genius. The pair also learn more about the Medici's fraught relationship with Michelangelo, as he fell from favour when he supported a rebellion against his own masters, and visit the world-famous Uffizi - including the Botticelli room(n)

programme.title: Britain's Pompeii: A Village Lost in Time
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts joins the team excavating a 3,000-year-old Bronze Age village in the Cambridgeshire Fens that burned to the ground and was preserved in peat. Archaeologists also investigate the cause of the fire. Was it just a terrible accident, or did the villagers' wealth provoke an attack?(n)

programme.title: The Prosecutors
programme.desc: The prosecutors deal with two violent domestic murders of women, who were killed by someone they knew, and the victim of an assault, Viv Driver-Hart, who is unhappy that her attacker has not been prosecuted. She has written to the CPS to appeal their decision under an new initiative, the Victims' Right to Review(n)

programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank charts the rise and fall of high-rise flats by following the history of one estate - the 19-storey Lincoln in Bow, east London, designed in 1960(n)

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In Italy, Mario, who's seven, spends Saturday cleaning prawns ready for customers at his dad's fish shop. On a hot day in Ghana, five-year-old Tyra cools off at the beach, and builds a massive sandcastle. And in Russia, seven-year-old Liza and her twin brother, Styopa, build a giant plastic tower, only to see it knocked down by their little sister Yulia. We see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and challenges of childhood are universal.(n)

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we meet a new family, five-year-old twins Daisy and Lily. They live near the seaside with their mum, dad and younger twin sisters Molly and Nell. The family has a busy breakfast time with all those mouths to feed, and Daisy and Lily are in charge of counting the eggs. Later, Mum takes the girls out for some fun on the beach where they make a picture using pebbles and seaweed. programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters have travelled to Horsetail Fall in the Yosemite National Park. It's a 300m-high waterfall made of melted snow. Once a year the setting sun lights up the water, making it glow like fire for just a few special minutes. This is called Firefall, and if there isn't enough water from the melting snow, Firefall doesn't happen. Unfortunately, Grandmaster Glitch has built a snow princess at the top of the fall and is using an ice machine to stop her melting. When the Go Jetters arrive to watch Firefall, they see that there is no water falling down! Can the team save snowy Princess Glorinda and Firefall?(n)

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are beside a river. They watch a fisherman make his rod longer and longer and longer. The fisherman casts out the line and then settles down for a nap. Chickedy and Chick run along the rod to the very end. Toodloo runs along the rod and jumps to join Chickedy and Chick. This causes the rod to spring up sending them flying! The fisherman wakes to discover a long, long, long fish at the end of his line. The Twirlywoo Screen draws a small line that Chickedy and Chick help make longer. Toodloo helps the line get a little longer still but when Great BigHoo taps the screen, the line becomes so long it shoots off the Twirlywoo Screen. programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: In south Wales, Kate and Teg set a mountain alight and experience the heat of a controlled burn with the local fire service in an area with the highest incidence of wild fires in the whole of the UK. Kate gets the creative juices flowing by lending a hand to the creation of an epic land art piece - a 30ft mandala - a stunning geometrically patterned circle made entirely from natural materials. Teg gets a run for her money, when meeting film actors at a rural Film Academy for Animals, and Kate fulfils her childhood dream with a master farrier, learning how to shoe a horse(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The body of a woman is found on train tracks in London, badly damaged from electrical burns. On the mortuary slab, the woman's recent and historic injuries suggest years of abuse, likely within the home. It is a difficult post-mortem for Nikki, as it stirs up troubling memories from her childhood. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Linda is humiliated when Mick describes her actions the previous night - but their argument is cut short by shocking news from Ollie's school. As if that were not enough for the troubled landlady, she also faces Sharon, who wants to know why she said Keanu was alive. Meanwhile, Lisa tells Phil she and Louise are moving to Portugal - a plan he opposes - Ian frantically tries to get his fellow residents to vote for him in the election, and Daniel and Jean run rings around Rainie and Stuart(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Serena needs to avoid causing trouble with an inspection looming, but when Jason has problems with the privatised porter company, she must decide if she can afford to help him. Nicky finds herself having to treat her own mother, who has been involved in a road accident, and is forced to take desperate measures to help her. Essie realises that she has to tell Ben everything about the illegal adoption if they are to stay together, but is not sure she can trust him with the truth(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. The investigation into Jenny's murder grinds to a halt when it emerges that she was dead before her body was left on the tracks, but the women at the refuge demand answers. Thomas makes some friends in high places who offer to help him achieve his political ambitions - but there is a price to be paid for their support(n)

programme.title: Handstand

Drama with Darren Evans, Mabli Jen Eustace, Christian Patterson and Ri Richards. Written and directed by Peter Watkins-Hughes(n)

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is never without her smartphone - social media connects her to her mates, keeps her up to date with celebrity gossip and it's even how she gets her news. Here, however, she wonders what affect it might be having on her self-esteem and mental health. To find out, Hayley attempts to give up her smartphone for two weeks to explore the impact that being switched on and connected 24 hours a day has on lives(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Asian Odyssey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Nadiya Hussain takes a trip to the Himalayan country of Nepal to learn more about its people, places and food as part of her adventure to explore her identity. She begins in Kathmandu, where she joins a supper club run by young foodies who share with her the vastly varied cuisine of their country, then heads to the historic city of Bhaktapur, which was devastated by the earthquake of 2015, and visits an abbey to meet nuns from a Buddhist sect who practise kung fu and teach female empowerment(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Tension mounts between Caitlin and Joe before they dramatically clash over Ruby, while Dylan is under pressure to cater for Scarlett's community Burns Supper event, and Angus is furious to discover Bob has left town without telling Kim(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. The investigation into Jenny's murder grinds to a halt when it emerges that she was dead before her body was left on the tracks, but the women at the refuge demand answers. Thomas makes some friends in high places who offer to help him achieve his political ambitions - but there is a price to be paid for their support(n)

programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Crystal Palace at Etihad Stadium and Watford v Tottenham Hotspur at Vicarage Road. The Citizens were looking to avoid a repeat of their shock 3-2 defeat in last season's corresponding fixture, while Spurs faced a tough task against a Watford side that has been rejuvenated by Nigel Pearson. Plus, Arsenal v Sheffield United, Brighton & Hove Albion v Aston Villa, Norwich City v Bournemouth, Southampton v Wolverhampton Wanderers, West Ham United v Everton and Newcastle United v Chelsea(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Winter Diaries
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Snowdonia to discover how golden eagles - one of Britain's rarest birds of prey - are poised to return to the skies of Wales after an absence of 200 years(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts explores a property up for auction in Mountain Ash, south Wales, while residences in Gillingham, and Walsall are also examined(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Gary Pe help two teams hunt for items at the Lincolnshire Antiques and Home show, while Eric Knowles explores the Japanese concept of wabi-sabi in pottery(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is in Kinross on the banks of Loch Leven, visiting local children's hospice Rachel House to see its inspirational work. Plus, Eddie Reader sings Amazing Grace(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine presents an appeal on behalf of Parkinson's UK, which funds research, support networks and specialised nurses to help those with the condition(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who have £500,000 to spend on a large family home in Northern Ireland where they can also keep animals and welcome paying guests. Away from the search, Nicki immerses herself in the country's maritime history at the dry dock where RMS Titanic was built(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a pair of memory filled gardens for widows Sue, who lives in Wallasey, Liverpool, and Anita, who resides in Fallowfield, Manchester(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, featuring a badly damaged suspended chair from the 1960s, a cast iron statue of Roman god Hercules, and a smokers' stand(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live on coral reefs, including groupers, small predators that scientists believe are as intelligent as chimpanzees. These creatures have a remarkable aptitude for communication to the point where they can interact with other species including octopi and work together to catch prey. Plus, footage of a vast spawning event involving several different species of fish, coral and invertebrates, and time-lapse photography of coral polyps growing and dying, creating a structure visible from space(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Ballycastle in Co Antrim, a coastal community that is becoming known for its crafts and artisan food and drink. Margherita Taylor heads to a farm that produces ethical and sustainable produce, Joe Crowley catches up with a couple who make award-winning smoked salmon and dulse seaweed, and Charlotte Smith goes on a red squirrel safari with a school group(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: It is 1903 and on the edge of Niagara Falls, something is wrong at Nikola Tesla's generator plant, where someone - or something - is sabotaging the maverick inventor's work. Has Tesla really received a message from Mars? And where does his great rival Thomas Edison fit into these events? The Doctor and her companions Yaz, Ryan and Graham must join forces with one of history's greatest minds to save both him and planet Earth. Guest starring Goran Visnjic, Robert Glenister and Anjli Mohindra(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Lucille is seconded to St Cuthbert's when a ward sister is off sick, but a lift malfunction sees her spend the evening with mother-to-be Connie instead of her planned rendezvous with Cyril. Nurse Crane tries to help a woman who is newly arrived from East Pakistan, while Shelagh enlists Violet, Trixie, Sister Frances and Valerie to put on a charity fashion show to raise money for the incubator fund(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Journalists from around the world descend on the Old Bailey as Stephen Ward's trial begins and the doctor trusts his friends in high places will come forward in support of his character. While Stephen faces the full force of the law and Mandy thrives on the publicity, Christine is terrified that her lie about Lucky Gordon will be revealed at the appeal. Starring Sophie Cookson and James Norton(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Liverpool v Manchester United at Anfield and Burnley v Leicester City at Turf Moor. Liverpool have been in dominant form this term, but United were the first team to take points off them this season when October's reverse fixture ended in a 1-1 draw, while Leicester will have been looking to capitalise on any slip-up from Jurgen Klopp's men with a victory over the Clarets(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Chelsea at Meadow Park and Birmingham City v Manchester City at SportNation.bet Stadium(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Adam Gemili, tennis star Heather Watson, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott and ex-West Ham United striker Dean Ashton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return with a week of live editions of the consumer programme, including the latest on the scandal of the insurance industry's car database that's giving some second-hand vehicles the all clear when they are actually insurance write-offs(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin looks at mid-terrace houses in Stoke-on-Trent and Wolverhampton and Martin Roberts explores a large listed building in Rochester(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: A Berkshire couple sample life in New Zealand for a week, but while Malay-born Roz longs to live in a warmer climate, her husband Chris is very cautious about such a major move(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shop worker is horrified when a knife-wielding man bursts into his store, leaving him with seconds to make the most important decision of his life(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma and Zara decide to learn sign language, while Al has bad news for Jimmi about the Icon. Rob notices his colleague Hailey Woodruff is becoming overly invested in a case(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicia and Mrs McCarthy are taken hostage when a midnight train robbery goes awry. Crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Sorcha Cusack and Nancy Carroll(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps an Anglo-Icelandic couple, who need to relocate from Reykjavik to Aberdeenshire on their £380,000 budget before their daughter starts university in the region(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two farming families hope their much-loved pygmy goats will bring them acclaim at the Mid Devon Show, but the competition is tough and there is a record number of goats in the ring(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and newcomer Steven Moore start their hunt in the Scottish Borders, buying items on their journey to sell at auction in Crooklands, near Kendal in the Lake District(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Getaways
programme.desc: Joe Lindsay and Jennifer Reoch explore Philadelphia in the USA, where they recreate Rocky's famous run up the steps at the Philadelphia Museum of Art in the boxing movie, visit the Liberty Bell, one of the most important symbols of American independence, and the Magic Gardens, where rubbish is turned into art. Joe also spends time at the jail that once held mobster Al Capone. In the short break, actor Atta Yaqub explores the best of Perthshire, including Scone Palace, the original home of the Stone of Destiny(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is mortified to realise she has forgotten Mick's birthday and quickly arranges a surprise party - and as everyone gathers in the pub to celebrate, she finds the temptation to have a drink hard to resist. Phil is celebrating his birthday too with a family meal and is relieved when Louise agrees to attend - only for her to lash out at him and Ben. Kat is thrilled when Whitney sets up a meeting with a new client, Rainie targets Jean and Daniel, and Ian tries to persuade Ruby to let him use the club for his election winner's party(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The body of a woman is found on train tracks in London, badly damaged from electrical burns. On the mortuary slab, the woman's recent and historic injuries suggest years of abuse, likely within the home. It is a difficult post-mortem for Nikki, as it stirs up troubling memories from her childhood. First of a two-part episode of the long-running crime drama following the work of the Lyell Centre's team of forensic pathology experts, starring Emilia Fox, David Caves, Richard Lintern and Liz Carr(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following the regulars at a West Belfast bingo hall, as they prepare for one of the busiest nights of the year - when the prize money rises to four figures. Among the players are Eilish, who learned the game at her grandmother's knee while in her teens, Margaret, who is juggling her job, her children, a college course, and her husband's health problems, and Kate, who is preparing to celebrate a friend's birthday(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following the Loughrans, who design and ship cutting-edge rally supercars all over the world from their base in the foothills of the Sperrin Mountains. The film follows a rally car's journey from order to delivery, meets a community with an insatiable need for speed and finds out how a former banker takes cars from the scrapheap and transforms them into rally supercars, while balancing all the pressures of family life(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: After a bad day at work, Liam talks to Jess and is forced to confront his mental health problems, leading him to remember the conflicting advice of two of his old teachers. At school he found little emotional support from PE teacher Mr Stones, but unconventional substitute teacher Miss Monroe made him try to channel his anger into more creative pursuits(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Hal Cruttenden hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, warming up the audience before comedy circuit regulars Justin Moorhouse and Tom Stade take the mic(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Michael B Jordan discuss starring in legal drama Just Mercy, while actress and comedian Jennifer Saunders talks about new mystery thriller series The Stranger, in which she features. Plus, Patrick Stewart reflects on his decision to reprise his most famous role in latest sci-fi spin-off Star Trek: Picard. Michael Kiwanuka performs Hero and more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)
